title: Rebase, Sequencer, Autosquash, and Topology Preservation · Graph Lens: Gita Rebase Onto Ranges
summary: A stacked change series must move between release bases, preserve selected merges, reorder dependent commits, and satisfy a review policy without losing authorship or test evidence. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita rebase onto ranges increment in rebase todo, mapping, and equivalence proof. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use --onto, upstream, branch, fork-point, and explicit revision sets to select exactly which commits move and where.
Model rebase as commit selection, temporary reset, ordered replay, new object creation, and ref movement rather than branch movement magic.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Gita Rebase Onto Ranges
steps.instruction:
theory case — Case G76 is a theory in a disposable repository. the rewrite preserved patch intent while changing parentage and commit identity. The learner must predict range-diff maps old and new patches and exposes reordered, amended, dropped, or new entries, run git range-diff refs/recovery/pre-rebase...HEAD, explain four patches map cleanly and one amended policy patch requires focused review, test this changed condition: drop a dependency patch in the scenario and observe the downstream mapping and behavior diverge, and preserve this recovery boundary: keep refs/recovery/pre-rebase until tree, tests, authorship, and publication review pass.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Case G76 is a worked example in a disposable repository. the rewrite preserved patch intent while changing parentage and commit identity. The learner must predict range-diff maps old and new patches and exposes reordered, amended, dropped, or new entries, run git range-diff refs/recovery/pre-rebase...HEAD, explain four patches map cleanly and one amended policy patch requires focused review, test this changed condition: drop a dependency patch in the scenario and observe the downstream mapping and behavior diverge, and preserve this recovery boundary: keep refs/recovery/pre-rebase until tree, tests, authorship, and publication review pass. A defensible solution applies Gita Rebase Onto Ranges, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to rebase todo, mapping, and equivalence proof.

content.code:
# Advanced Git 2.55 deterministic scenario 2
# before-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# hypothesis: the rewrite preserved patch intent while changing parentage and commit identity
git range-diff refs/recovery/pre-rebase...HEAD
# expected: range-diff maps old and new patches and exposes reordered, amended, dropped, or new entries
# observation: four patches map cleanly and one amended policy patch requires focused review
# changed-case: drop a dependency patch in the scenario and observe the downstream mapping and behavior diverge
# after-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# tree-proof: compare the exact affected tree and paths before judging success
# behavior-proof: retain a changed-case test or invariant that can falsify the Git-only conclusion
# recovery: keep refs/recovery/pre-rebase until tree, tests, authorship, and publication review pass
# transfer-limit: coordinate real shared-history rewrites and publish with an explicit lease expectation
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

steps.title: Build Gita Rebase Onto Ranges evidence
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-rebase-onto-ranges-riq0ot" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Rebase Onto Ranges into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-rebase-onto-ranges-riq0ot
# scenario-key: trace-riq0ot probe-to0qir boundary-q0otri owner-riq0otx fault-riq0otz
# before-ref: main=a11ceri candidate=b00b1ot
# hypothesis: the rewrite preserved patch intent while changing parentage and commit identity
git range-diff refs/recovery/pre-rebase...HEAD
# expected: range-diff maps old and new patches and exposes reordered, amended, dropped, or new entries
# observation: four patches map cleanly and one amended policy patch requires focused review
# changed-case: drop a dependency patch in the scenario and observe the downstream mapping and behavior diverge
# after-ref: main=a11ceri candidate=c0ffeet
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: keep refs/recovery/pre-rebase until tree, tests, authorship, and publication review pass
# transfer-limit: coordinate real shared-history rewrites and publish with an explicit lease expectation
